L1.area: String stream buffer management and byte manipulation operations

L2.description: A buffer overread vulnerability exists where certain byte ungetting operations on a string-based I/O stream incorrectly calculate the expansion size of an internal buffer. When the stream's internal position is past the end of the underlying string, the miscalculation allows a subsequent read operation to return memory contents beyond the allocated string boundary.

L3.area: Native C extension implementing string-based I/O stream operations, specifically the byte ungetting and buffer expansion logic
L3.description: A buffer overread vulnerability exists in the native C extension for string-based I/O streams where the byte ungetting operations miscalculate the buffer expansion size when the stream position exceeds the underlying string length. A crafted sequence of unget operations followed by a read can return memory values past the end of the allocated string buffer.

ground_truth.reason: The strio_unget_bytes function miscalculates the buffer expansion size when the stream position is beyond the string length, causing ungetbyte/ungetc to read past the end of the string buffer and a subsequent StringIO.gets call to leak memory contents.
